title: Pasta with aubergine
description: A simple Sicilian pasta dish where aubergine, ripe tomatoes, basil and salted ricotta combine into a sublime summer meal.
context: Sicilian Summer: An Adventure in Cooking with My Grandsons by Mary Taylor Simeti (Silverwood).
steps: Wash the aubergines and cut them, unpeeled, into 5mm slices (Palermo style) or into finger-size sticks. Sprinkle with abundant salt and allow them to dry for a couple of hours. Rinse off the salt, drain, pat dry, and fry the aubergines in lots of olive oil until golden brown on all sides. Drain on kitchen paper.
Saute the garlic cloves and the tomatoes together with very little salt and the pepper in about 5 tbsp olive oil for about 15 minutes.

Cook the pasta in abundant boiling salted water until al dente, then drain. Toss it in a serving bowl with half the ricotta and the basil. Spoon the aubergine and tomato mixture on top and sprinkle with the rest of the ricotta.
